steps.title: Read Schmidt's fake exit
steps.content: After the narrator's wife says she does not find art compelling, Schmidt winces, sighs, and invents an appointment. The narrator makes clear that no real appointment exists, so the exit is a staged reaction.

steps.title: Infer the indirect reaction
steps.content: A overgeneralizes about when Schmidt acts theatrically, C treats the false appointment as real absentmindedness, and D invents an earlier argument about a painting. Schmidt communicates disapproval through behavior instead of words. The answer is B.
